ops.title: Assignment-captured web dumps are programmatic sinks — never rewrite
ops.body: `VAR=$(curl …)` output is consumed programmatically by the calling code, not read by a human. Rewriting the curl to `ccx web read` (readability-extracted markdown, no raw-HTML mode exists) silently corrupts every downstream probe — the 2026-07-19 incident: `H=$(curl -sL <url>)` followed by `echo "$H" | grep -c '<marker>'` returned meaningless zeros. Hence `assignment_captured()` in web_guards.py: an assignment-hosted page dump is allowed untouched (no rewrite, no block). The capture-then-redump hole (`H=$(curl …); echo "$H"`) is accepted by explicit user decision — no taint tracking. Boundary: only provable assignment heads get the sink lane; test-expression hosts (`[ -n "$(…)" ]`) and span-less string-embedded substitutions (`echo "$(curl …)"`) keep the normal rewrite/block lane — the released cc-transcript parser surfaces the latter span-less, and unreleased v8 folds them entirely (commit abe83002 pins released behavior).
